b:T1a83b,<div class="specification">
<p>Squid have been used for research on nerve impulses. The mechanism of nerve transmission in these animals is the same as that in humans. The image shows an oscilloscope trace from a nerve impulse in a squid’s giant axon.</p>
<p style="text-align: center;"><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></p>
<p style="text-align: center;">[squid] Pixabay.</p>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Estimate the resting potential for this axon.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Outline the role of the sodium–potassium pump in maintaining the resting potential.</p>
<div class="marks">[2]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div>2:["$","article",null,{"className":"q-page","children":[["$","p",null,{"style":{"margin":0},"children":["$","$L6",null,{"href":"/subject/biology/topic/6/","children":["← back to ","Biology"," topic ","6"]}]}],["$","h1",null,{"children":"21M.2.SL.TZ1.4"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-meta","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"tag","children":"pestle"}],["$","span",null,{"children":"Biology"}],null,["$","span",null,{"children":"SL"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["Paper ","2"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"21M"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["· ","6-5-neurons-and-synapses"]}],["$","a",null,{"href":"https://pestle.pages.dev/app/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener","children":"source ↗"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-content","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$b"}}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Markscheme / solution"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>−65 <span style=\"text-decoration:underline;\">mV</span> ✔</p>\n<p><em>Unit required; minus sign required. Accept answers from −62mV to −65mV</em>.</p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">a.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>a. sodium/potassium pump pumps sodium ions Na<sup>+</sup> out of axon and pumps potassium ions/K<sup>+</sup> in ✔</p>\n<p>b. requires energy/ATP/against concentration gradients/active transport ✔</p>\n<p>c. three sodium ions pumped out for every two potassium ions pumped in ✔</p>\n<p>d. results in charge difference between inside and outside where outside is positive relative to inside ✔</p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">b.</div>\n</div>"}}]]}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Examiners’ report"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n[N/A]\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">a.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>Strong students answered this question showing clear understanding of the process. However, most students showed poor understanding with errors: correct direction of ions being pumped not known; the direction of ions during repolarization was mixed up with resting state. Few wrote about the charge difference between inside and outside of the axon during the resting potential.</p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">b.</div>\n</div>"}}]]}]]}]
